description:
Goal: keep contract/body/dispatch/keying alive after the source-authority swap by deriving an honest legacy_fn_def-style downstream artifact from function quoted roots, not from the old parser or Rc<Item>/FnDef authority.

Grounding:
- src/compiler2/jobs/contract.rs, src/compiler2/jobs/body.rs, src/compiler2/jobs/dispatch.rs, and src/compiler2/jobs/keying.rs all read world.function_definition(function).ast today.
- Those jobs currently consume Expr/Pattern/FnDef directly, but compiler2 has no typed quoted-source reader layer for function bodies and patterns yet.
- Reusing the old parser inside compiler2 would violate the source-authority goal of fz-rh2.11.7.9, but deleting FnDef overnight would strand the downstream phases.

Signal:
- compiler2 never reparses source text through the old frontend in order to feed contract/body/dispatch/keying
- downstream jobs obtain an explicitly named legacy_fn_def artifact (or equivalent honest carrier) derived from the function quoted root
- function/body/dispatch semantic facts remain stable when the derived downstream artifact is semantically unchanged, even if the quoted source heap/root changes elsewhere
- naming/docs make it obvious this is transitional legacy surface, not canonical source authority

Change:
- add a one-way compiler2-owned quoted->legacy function artifact derivation path
- cache or fact it at the function tier so downstream jobs share one derived artifact instead of each growing their own ad hoc reader
- route contract/body/dispatch/keying through that artifact while keeping quoted source as the only authoritative source record
- add focused tests around revision behavior and no-old-parser usage

Why:
- this preserves one source authority while buying a clean migration seam for the deepest remaining old-AST consumers
- the stinky name is intentional: we should be able to grep for the debt later and rip it out deliberately
title: Compiler2 legacy_fn_def: derive downstream fn input from quoted roots
